title: Street-Style Dahi Vada

ingredients.name: urad dal (husked, soaked for 4–6 hours or overnight)

subs.name: moong dal (split, husked) soaked for only 2 hours makes lighter, quicker vadas with a slightly milder flavour
thick coconut curd seasoned the same way for a dairy-free version
2 tablespoons of ready-made tamarind concentrate thinned with a teaspoon of water

steps.text: Soak the urad dal in plenty of cold water for at least 4 hours or overnight before you begin. Drain and rinse thoroughly. Grind the soaked dal in a wet grinder or blender with as little water as possible — add water by the tablespoon only as needed — until you have a thick, airy, perfectly smooth batter with no grainy texture. Beat the batter vigorously with a spoon for 2 minutes to incorporate air; it should look slightly fluffy. Season with salt, ginger, green chilli, and hing.

Pour oil into a deep kadai to 8 cm depth and heat to 170°C over medium flame. Wet your hand with water, scoop about 2 tablespoons of batter, and gently slip it into the oil as a rough round shape. Fry 4–5 vadas at a time for 5–6 minutes, turning twice, until they are pale golden and cooked through — they should feel firm on the outside. Do not colour them too deeply. Drain on paper towels.

Transfer the fried vadas immediately into a large bowl of hot water (not boiling — just off the heat). Let them soak for 8–10 minutes until they have absorbed water and become noticeably softer and expanded. Gently press each vada between your palms to squeeze out excess water — they should be soft but not waterlogged. Place on a serving dish.

Whisk the curd with sugar, a pinch of salt, and half the roasted cumin until completely smooth and well seasoned. Make the green chutney by blending mint leaves, coriander leaves, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt with 3 tablespoons of water. Taste both preparations and adjust salt and tang.

To serve, spoon the sweetened curd generously over the vadas so they are fully covered. Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney in stripes over the curd. Sprinkle the remaining cumin, red chilli powder, and chaat masala over the top. Chill for at least 15 minutes before serving — dahi vada is at its best when the vadas are cold, the curd is icy, and the chutneys have soaked in slightly.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: urad-dal → moong dal (split, husked) soaked for only 2 hours makes lighter, quicker vadas with a slightly milder flavour; curd → thick coconut curd seasoned the same way for a dairy-free version; tamarind → 2 tablespoons of ready-made tamarind concentrate thinned with a teaspoon of water
